text: Photons knock electrons loose in silicon — that's the photovoltaic effect. But what does that really mean for energy generation? In a solar panel, sunlight hits silicon cells, exciting electrons. These moving electrons create an electric current. And here’s the kicker: it’s all about the material! Silicon’s unique properties make it a superstar in solar tech. Curious about renewable energy? Hit follow for more!
